req.title: Standing order (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A customer shall set up a standing order with start date in the future; past start dates shall be rejected. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

doc.reasoning: The requirement has three distinct testable behaviours: (1) a valid future start_date creates a standing order (EP positive path), (2) a start_date <= today is rejected with 422 (NEG/BVA), and (3) a duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key returns the original response unchanged (STC idempotency). BVA is applied around the today/tomorrow boundary for start_date. DT covers the combined decision of valid-date × duplicate-key. NEG covers the past-date and today-date rejection paths. Together these achieve full branch coverage of the stated requirement.

tests.name: test_create_standing_order_future_date_returns_201
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and auth_headers fixture is available
start_date is set to today + 1 day (clearly within valid partition)
actions: POST /standing-orders with a valid payload including start_date = today + 1 day
Capture HTTP status code and response body
expected: Response body 'start_date' matches the submitted value
trace: R-FIN-095 – 'A customer shall set up a standing order with start date in the future'
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta
import uuid

def test_create_standing_order_future_date_returns_201(auth_headers, api_client):
    start_date = (date.today() + timedelta(days=1)).isoformat()
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "amount": {"value": "100.00", "currency": "GBP"},
        "frequency": "MONTHLY",
        "start_date": start_date,
        "creditor_account": "<valid_creditor_account>"
    }
    headers = {**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}
    resp = api_client.post("/standing-orders", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("order_id"), "order_id must be present and non-empty"
    assert body["start_date"] == start_date
    assert body["status"] == "scheduled"

tests.name: test_create_standing_order_past_date_returns_422
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and auth_headers fixture is available
start_date is set to today - 1 day (clearly within invalid partition)
actions: Capture HTTP status code and response body
expected: Response body contains a validation error referencing 'start_date'
trace: R-FIN-095 – 'past start dates shall be rejected'
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta
import uuid

def test_create_standing_order_past_date_returns_422(auth_headers, api_client):
    start_date = (date.today() - timedelta(days=1)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "amount": {"value": "100.00", "currency": "GBP"},
        "frequency": "MONTHLY",
        "start_date": start_date,
        "creditor_account": "<valid_creditor_account>"
    }
    headers = {**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": str(uuid.uuid4())}
    resp = api_client.post("/standing-orders", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "start_date" in error_text, "Error body must reference start_date field"

tests.name: test_create_standing_order_today_boundary_returns_422
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and auth_headers fixture is available
start_date is exactly today (the boundary value start_date == today must be rejected)
actions: Capture HTTP status code and response body
expected: Response body contains a validation error referencing 'start_date'
trace: R-FIN-095 – 'Error pathways: 422 when start_date <= today' — boundary is start_date == today
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date
import uuid

def test_create_standing_order_today_boundary_returns_422(auth_headers, api_client):
    start_date = date.today().isoformat()
    payload = {
        "amount": {"value": "100.00", "currency": "GBP"},
        "frequency": "MONTHLY",
        "start_date": start_date,
        "creditor_account": "<valid_creditor_account>"
    }
    headers = {**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": str(uuid.uuid4())}
    resp = api_client.post("/standing-orders", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "start_date" in error_text, "Error body must reference start_date field"

tests.name: test_duplicate_idempotency_key_returns_original_response
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and auth_headers fixture is available
A unique Idempotency-Key is generated for this test run
start_date is set to today + 7 days
actions: POST /standing-orders with a valid payload and Idempotency-Key (first submission)
Assert 201 on the first response and record order_id
POST /standing-orders again with identical payload and same Idempotency-Key (second submission)
Compare status code and order_id of both responses
expected: First POST returns HTTP 201 with a valid order_id
Second POST returns HTTP 200 or 201 (server-defined idempotent replay code)
order_id in second response equals order_id in first response
No duplicate standing order is created
trace: R-FIN-095 – 'The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result'
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta
import uuid

def test_duplicate_idempotency_key_returns_original_response(auth_headers, api_client):
    start_date = (date.today() + timedelta(days=7)).isoformat()
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "amount": {"value": "50.00", "currency": "GBP"},
        "frequency": "WEEKLY",
        "start_date": start_date,
        "creditor_account": "<valid_creditor_account>"
    }
    headers = {**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}
    resp1 = api_client.post("/standing-orders", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp1.status_code == 201
    order_id_1 = resp1.json()["order_id"]
    resp2 = api_client.post("/standing-orders", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp2.status_code in (200, 201)
    order_id_2 = resp2.json()["order_id"]
    assert order_id_1 == order_id_2, "Idempotent replay must return the original order_id"

tests.name: test_decision_table_invalid_date_overrides_valid_key
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and auth_headers fixture is available
start_date is today - 10 days (invalid condition column)
actions: POST /standing-orders with past start_date and a valid Idempotency-Key
expected: No order is created or stored
trace: R-FIN-095 – DT row: valid-key=TRUE, valid-date=FALSE → outcome=422 rejection
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta
import uuid

def test_decision_table_invalid_date_overrides_valid_key(auth_headers, api_client):
    start_date = (date.today() - timedelta(days=10)).isoformat()
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "amount": {"value": "200.00", "currency": "GBP"},
        "frequency": "MONTHLY",
        "start_date": start_date,
        "creditor_account": "<valid_creditor_account>"
    }
    headers = {**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}
    resp = api_client.post("/standing-orders", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "order_id" not in body, "No order_id must be returned on 422"
